text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the structure of existing code without changing its behavior. When developers refactor, they focus on making the code cleaner, more readable, and easier to maintain. This might involve renaming variables, breaking large functions into smaller ones, or removing duplicated logic. Refactoring does not add new features or fix bugs—its goal is to improve the internal quality of the code. Over time, if code is not refactored, it accumulates technical debt, which means it becomes messy and harder to work with. Regular refactoring helps reduce this debt, making future changes faster and less risky. Ultimately, it saves time and improves collaboration within a team.
